mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in FEDFUNDS signals an easing impulse that lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if FEDFUNDS falls but subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative or if the sign of the relationship is inverted in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is already high, real-rate pressure tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than positive ones, or if the joint effect vanishes once FEDFUNDS is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE is a late signal of weakening labor demand and slower consumer income growth, so a 3-month increase in unemployment should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment rises by the stated amount but 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not negative in a majority of cases or if the effect reverses in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE confirms labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption expectations and corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS down / UNRATE up pattern does not outperform either variable alone in predicting negative 20-day returns, or if triggered periods yield positive returns more often than negative ones.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and near-term revenue momentum for large-cap firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive versus its prior 3-month trend
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend changes are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal loses predictive power after a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior contraction signals stabilization in rate-sensitive demand and housing-linked activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts rebounds are not followed by positive 60-day returns in a majority of cases or if the effect disappears outside rate-cutting periods.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO together with low inflation indicates stronger real output without acute price pressure, which supports profit margins and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL remains below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-INDPRO / low-CPI condition does not outperform a random baseline for 20-day returns, or if inflation-accelerating episodes reverse the sign of the effect.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior expansion indicates weakening aggregate demand that will eventually feed into earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than negative ones, or if the relationship vanishes in rolling 3-year tests.
